MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-09-2022 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-04-2021 Continued To Mar-14-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Michelle Leaos Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-15-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Corey Page, Esq. (415-637-2354; cpage@evansandpage.com) of Evans & Page for Plaintiff.
Ciara Dineen, Esq. (415-362-2580; Ciara.Dineen@lewisbrisbois.com) of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MICHELLE LEAO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT:

Defendant Michelle Leao's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant meets her burden as the moving party to show that it is undisputed that the action has no merit.

[see word minutes for details.]

order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. =(302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Michelle Leaos Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-02-2021 Continued To Jul-15-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MICHELLE LEAO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Matter is continued to July 15, 2021 to be heard by Judge Schulman.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR No. 7855, Email: eric.throne.csr@gmail.com, Tel: 707-305-8964 (302/RBU)

Defendant Michelle Leao?S Amended Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Apr-05-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Apperances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Corey Page, Esq. of Evans & Page at cpage@evansandpage.com, 415.896.5072, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Ciara Dineen, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard Smith LLP at Ciara.Dineen@lewisbrisbois.com, 415.362.2580, appearing for the Defendant.

DEFENDANT MICHELLE LEAO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Matter called, ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied.

Defendant's motion is timely. "No motion may be made pursuant to this section if a pretrial conference order has been entered pursuant to Section 575, or within 30 days of the date the action is initially set for trial, whichever is later, unless the court otherwise permits." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 438.) No pretrial conference order has been entered into here.
A motion for judgment on the pleadings may be made on the ground that the "complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against that defendant." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 438(c)(3)(B)(ii).) "The grounds for motion provided for in this section shall appear on the face of the challenged pleading or from any matter of which the court is required to take judicial notice." (Id. sec. 438(d).) Defendant does not show that in order to plead a claim under Health & Safety Code section 123110, Plaintiff must plead facts that show bad faith under section 123115. Defendant provides no support for such a pleading requirement or for her interpretation of the statutes at issue. Plaintiff's allegations that his request "for his daughter's records" was denied bv Defendant are sufficient. (See Compl. 33-34.) "[T]he complaint need only allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action; each evidentiary fact that might eventually form part of the plaintiff's proof need not be alleged." (C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 872.)
As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
